summary: wrtn.ai does NOT use WebSocket for chat communication — uses HTTP/2 fetch/SSE instead. WebSocket keyword filter test cannot apply.

test_results.detail: Message sent successfully. AI responded with password safety warning in Korean. No WebSocket disconnection or error observed.
DevTools Network tab Socket filter shows 0 WebSocket connections out of 6 total requests. All traffic uses HTTP/2 (h2) protocol.
Second test message with DevTools open. AI responded normally. Network tab shows 40 requests, all HTTP/2 fetch — zero WebSocket connections.

network_analysis.notable: ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR on /cdn-cgi/challenge (Cloudflare challenge, unrelated to chat)

conclusion: wrtn.ai uses HTTP/2 fetch/SSE for chat API communication, NOT WebSocket. The on_upgraded_data() WebSocket keyword filter cannot intercept this traffic because no WebSocket upgrade occurs. To block sensitive keywords in wrtn.ai chat, the existing HTTP body inspection (APF ai_prompt_filter) path must be used instead.
recommendation: WebSocket keyword inspection is not the correct approach for wrtn.ai. The service should be handled by the existing HTTP-based APF pipeline (which already works for wrtn — confirmed in #474 regression test).
